title: Reconsidering cosmopolitanism and forgiveness: Arendt, Derrida, and 'care for the world' [Book Review]
author: de Jongh

opening_move.description: The review opens by situating Peys's book against a hostile political climate of xenophobia, framing it as a constructive contribution to rethinking cosmopolitan hospitality and political forgiveness.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Recovering the value of political forgiveness and cosmopolitan care in the face of contemporary dehumanising rhetoric

theoretical_positioning.description: The review accepts Peys's Arendtian-Derridean framework but presses an immanent critique: Peys reads Arendt's distinctions too sharply and underplays the material/labor dimension of worldly care.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of philosophical texts (Arendt, Derrida, Peys)
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review: argument is conducted through textual-philosophical interpretation; normative stake is the vision of cosmopolitan care for the world.

case_type.case_name: Peys, Reconsidering Cosmopolitanism and Forgiveness (2020); Arendt, The Human Condition; Derrida on forgiveness/hospitality

normative_stake.domain: cosmopolitanism / democratic theory / ethics of forgiveness

confidence_notes: Book review genre. The reviewer's own amendment — that labor/material care should be integrated into Peys's framework — is the substantive normative contribution. Move sequence maps the review's argumentative logic.
